businessModel.text: Revenue from Space Systems (satellites, classified payloads, ~35%), Aeronautics (B-21, B-2, 30%), Missiles & Fire Control (GMLRS, Lance, 20%), and Mission Systems (C4ISR, cyber, 15%). Almost exclusively US and allied government contracts.

recentCatalysts.text:
Sentinel (GBSD) ICBM programme: $75bn+ contract; production lines fully funded FY26
    B-21 Raider: Low-rate initial production underway; 5 test aircraft in flight
    Classified space intelligence programme revenue grew 22% FY24
    NASA Artemis III lunar programme: $3.4bn contract award for EGS lunar terrain vehicle
items: Sentinel (GBSD) ICBM programme: $75bn+ contract; production lines fully funded FY26
B-21 Raider: Low-rate initial production underway; 5 test aircraft in flight
Classified space intelligence programme revenue grew 22% FY24
NASA Artemis III lunar programme: $3.4bn contract award for EGS lunar terrain vehicle

risks.risk: 1. Single-prime supplier status creates political scrutiny (anti-competitive concerns)
2. Sentinel programme technical complexity: first test launch delayed to FY27
3. Fire-sale of VSMPOL space systems division (divested Q4 FY24) — signals management uncertainty
4. DoD budget sensitivity: space spending competes with shipbuilding for appropriations
5. Labour and materials inflation on fixed-price contracts constrains margin improvement

entryExit.text:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $619.83–$687.2 — current levels are fairly valued.
Trim / Take Profits above $687.2 — approaching the 52-week high at $774.00.
Stop-loss: A sustained close below $441.13 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
